name: Deploy Azure Functions from the CLI
description: Create a serverless function app and deploy functions using Azure CLI and Azure Functions Core Tools.

steps.title: Create a resource group and storage account
steps.command: az group create --name func-rg --location eastus && az storage account create --name funcstore$RANDOM --resource-group func-rg --sku Standard_LRS
steps.description: Functions require a storage account for triggers and state management.

steps.title: Create the function app
steps.command: az functionapp create --resource-group func-rg --consumption-plan-location eastus --runtime node --runtime-version 20 --functions-version 4 --name myfunc-unique --storage-account funcstore
steps.description: Creates a consumption-plan function app that scales automatically and bills per execution.
steps.tip: Consumption plan is free for the first 1 million executions per month.

steps.title: Initialize a local function project
steps.description: Scaffolds a local project with the required configuration files.

steps.title: Create a function
steps.command: cd MyFuncProject && func new --name HttpTrigger --template "HTTP trigger"
steps.description: Adds an HTTP-triggered function to the project.

steps.title: Deploy to Azure
steps.description: Packages and deploys all functions in the project to the remote function app.

fullScript:
# Deploy Azure Functions from CLI
az group create --name func-rg --location eastus
az storage account create --name funcstore --resource-group func-rg --sku Standard_LRS
az functionapp create --resource-group func-rg --consumption-plan-location eastus --runtime node --runtime-version 20 --functions-version 4 --name myfunc-unique --storage-account funcstore
func init MyFuncProject --worker-runtime node --language javascript
cd MyFuncProject
func new --name HttpTrigger --template "HTTP trigger"
func azure functionapp publish myfunc-unique

faq.question: What is the difference between Consumption and Premium plans?
faq.answer: Consumption scales to zero and bills per execution. Premium keeps instances warm for lower latency and supports VNet integration, but has a baseline cost.

faq.question: Can I test functions locally before deploying?
faq.answer: Yes. Run 'func start' in your project directory to start a local runtime. Functions are available at http://localhost:7071/api/<function-name>.
